 {"id":"bd-7h7","title":"bd init should stop running daemon to avoid stale cache","description":"When running bd init, any running daemon continues with stale cached data, causing bd stats and other commands to show old counts.

Repro:
1. Have daemon running with 788 issues cached
2. Clean JSONL to 128 issues, delete db, run bd init
3. bd stats still shows 788 (daemon cache)
4. Must manually run bd daemon --stop

Fix: bd init should automatically stop any running daemon before reinitializing.","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T13:26:47.117226-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}

This would allow bd doctor to detect when migrations are needed and suggest running them.

 {"id":"bd-7pwh","title":"HOP-compatible schema additions","description":"Add optional fields to Beads schema to enable future HOP integration.
All fields are backwards-compatible (optional, omitted if empty).

## Reference
See ~/gt/docs/hop/BEADS-SCHEMA-CHANGES.md for full specification.

## P1 Changes (Must Have Before Launch)

### 1. EntityRef type
Structured entity reference that can become HOP URI:
```go
type EntityRef struct {
    Name     string // "polecat/Nux"
    Platform string // "gastown"
    Org      string // "steveyegge"  
    ID       string // "polecat-nux"
}
```

### 2. creator field
Every issue tracks who created it (EntityRef).

### 3. assignee_ref field
Structured form alongside existing string assignee.

### 4. validations array
Track who validated work completion:
```go
type Validation struct {
    Validator *EntityRef
    Outcome   string    // accepted, rejected, revision_requested
    Timestamp time.Time
    Score     *float32  // Future
}
```

## P2 Changes (Should Have)

### 5. work_type field
"mutex" (default) or "open_competition"

### 6. crystallizes field
Boolean - does this work compound (true) or evaporate (false)?

### 7. cross_refs field
Array of URIs to beads in other repos:
- "beads://github/anthropics/claude-code/bd-xyz"

## P3 Changes (Nice to Have)

### 8. skill_vector placeholder
Reserved for future embeddings: []float32

## Implementation Notes
- All fields optional in JSONL serialization
- Empty/null fields omit from output
- No migration needed for existing data
- CLI additions: --creator, --validated-by filters","notes":"Scope reduced after review. P1 only: EntityRef type, creator field, validations array. Deferred: assignee_ref, work_type, crystallizes, cross_refs, skill_vector (YAGNI - semantics unclear, can add later when needed).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-22T02:42:39.267984-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T20:09:09.211821-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T20:09:09.211821-08:00","close_reason":"HOP P1 schema additions complete: EntityRef type, Creator field, Validations array. P2/P3 items deferred to bd-zt59."}

 {"id":"bd-f616","title":"Digest: Version Bump: test-squash","description":"## Molecule Execution Summary

**Molecule**: Version Bump: test-squash
**Steps**: 8

**Completed**: 0/8

---

### Steps

1. **[open]** Verify release artifacts
   Check GitHub releases page - binaries for darwin/linux/windows should be available

2. **[open]** Commit and push release
   git add -A && git commit && git push to trigger CI

3. **[open]** Update CHANGELOG.md with release notes
   Add meaningful release notes to CHANGELOG.md describing what changed in test-squash

4. **[open]** Wait for CI to pass
   Monitor GitHub Actions - all checks must pass before release artifacts are built

5. **[open]** Restart running daemons
   Kill and restart any running bd daemons to pick up new version: pkill -f 'bd daemon' && bd daemon --start

6. **[open]** Update local installation
   Run install script or brew upgrade to get new version locally: curl -fsSL .../install.sh | bash

7. **[open]** Run bump-version.sh test-squash
   Run ./scripts/bump-version.sh test-squash to update version in all files

8. **[open]** Update info.go versionChanges
   Add entry to versionChanges in cmd/bd/info.go with agent-actionable changes for test-squash

 {"id":"bd-f7p1","title":"Add tests for mol spawn --attach","description":"Code review (bd-obep) found no tests for the spawn --attach functionality.

**Test cases needed:**
1. Basic attach - spawn proto with one --attach
2. Multiple attachments - spawn with --attach A --attach B
3. Attach types - verify sequential vs parallel bonding
4. Error case: attaching non-proto (missing template label)
5. Variable aggregation - vars from primary + attachments combined
6. Dry-run output includes attachment info

**Implementation notes:**
- Tests should use in-memory storage
- Create test protos, spawn with attachments, verify dependency structure
- Check that sequential uses 'blocks' type, parallel uses 'parent-child'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:58:16.766461-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:12.136215-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T21:33:12.136215-08:00","close_reason":"Added 6 tests for mol spawn --attach: basic attach, multiple attachments, sequential/parallel bond types, non-proto validation, variable aggregation, and dry-run output","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-f7p1","depends_on_id":"bd-obep","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T10:58:16.767616-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-fa1q","title":"bd swarm create command","description":"Add bd swarm create to create a swarm molecule from an epic.

## Context
Swarms ARE persistent molecules - not separate JSON state. This command creates
a swarm molecule that:
- Links to the epic it orchestrates
- Has mol_type=swarm
- Specifies a coordinator
- Can be discovered by any agent

## Core Principle: Discovery Over Tracking
The swarm molecule doesn't track step-by-step state. Instead:
- Query bd ready --parent=<epic> to find the ready front
- Query bd list --parent=<epic> --status=closed to find completed
- Derive progress from children's statuses

## Implementation
1. New command: bd swarm create <epic-id> [--coordinator=<addr>]
2. Validate epic structure first (call bd swarm validate)
3. Create molecule bead:
   - mol_type: swarm
   - epic: <epic-id>
   - coordinator: <addr>
   - status: open
4. Return the swarm molecule ID

## Single-Issue Wrapping
If given a single issue (not epic), auto-wrap:
- Create an epic with that issue as child
- Then create swarm molecule

## Reference
~/gt/docs/swarm-architecture.md - 'Swarm Structure' section

## Acceptance
- bd swarm create <epic> creates a swarm molecule
- Molecule has mol_type=swarm
- Links to epic via epic field
- Validates structure before creation","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:11:35.067432-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-28T21:25:01.522368-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fa1q","depends_on_id":"bd-oxgi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:11:52.428038-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-fa1q","depends_on_id":"bd-nkqx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:11:52.446532-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
